title: EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE - 211info
text: POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH WEST SALEM EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rapid identification, support, assessment, and treatment for the early signs of psychosis. Services include education about psychosis, medication along with medication monitoring, individual and group counseling, multi-family groups, occupational therapy, family education and support, vocational training and life-skills coaching, crisis services, and connections to community resources. 1520 Plaza Street NW Suite 150 Salem, OR 97304 Eligibility: Ages 12-30 who have had a first episode of psychosis within the last 12 months or who are experiencing early at-risk symptoms for psychosis and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm (503) 385-7417 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 585-3012 Main phone (503) 623-9289 Crisis Line Weekdays (800) 560-5535 Crisis Line Evenings and weekends Crisis Line/Referrals (503) 385-7417 Weekdays Crisis Hotline (800) 560-5833 Evenings and weekends Crisis Line (800) 560-5535 Evenings and weekends INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for intake FEES: Accepts OHP and private insurances. Sliding scale fees available for uninsured patients. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable. Proof of income if applying for sliding scale fees. AREA SERVED: Polk County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: The clinic is on the street behind Safeway on Edgewater in West Salem. If facing Safeway, take the street to the right (Rosemont Avenue) and then turn left on Plaza. Services: Early Intervention for Mental Illness Other Services Offered At This Location: MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - ADULT BEHAVIORAL HEALTH SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - CRISIS HOTLINE AND INTERVENTION MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - YOUTH AND FAMILY MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
